prose: Fukui reactivity indices computed for furan (9 atoms: O, C, C, C, C, H, H, H, H) using DFT at tier 'standard' (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density-fit, PySCF RKS), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1 (closed-shell singlet). The N±1 states used cation (charge +1, mult 2) and anion (charge −1, mult 2). Partial charges computed via Mulliken population analysis (PySCF). Geometry: user-supplied furan.xyz, single-point (no re-optimization). All SCFs converged; charge conservation checks passed (sum(f+)=1.0000, sum(f-)=1.0000). Integrity: trustworthy=true. No engine warnings reported. The most electrophilic atom is C5 (f+ = 1.9242931) and the most nucleophilic is also C5 (f- = 0.1616371) by the engine's report. Full per-atom Fukui indices: f+ = [-1.3316182, 1.2286866, 1.2270264, 1.9233053, 1.9242931, -1.2190639, -1.2191056, -0.76678308, -0.76674059]; f- = [0.08382155, 0.089103093, 0.089304821, 0.161527, 0.1616371, 0.10248412, 0.1025135, 0.10481615, 0.10479266]; dual descriptor = [-1.4154398, 1.1395835, 1.1377216, 1.7617783, 1.762656, -1.3215481, -1.321619, -0.87159923, -0.87153325]. Plot saved to: /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/furan/argo_claude-sonnet-4.6/20260703-194113_furan_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png. Note: negative Mulliken Fukui values on O and H atoms are a known artifact of the Mulliken scheme with polarized basis sets.
